profile.name: Dr. Kevin Knuth
roles: Professor of Physics, University at Albany (SUNY)
Former Research Scientist, NASA Ames Research Center
organizations: IFEX (Interdisciplinary Research Centre for Extraterrestrial Studies)
profile.summary: Physicist and Bayesian inference specialist whose 2019 peer-reviewed paper in Entropy calculated flight characteristics of documented UAP cases, finding accelerations of 100 to 1,000+ g with no sonic booms or thermal signatures. Leads UAPx, which conducted the first academic instrumented field expedition for UAP detection, and is the lead author on the 2025 collaborative 'New Science of UAP' paper in Progress in Aerospace Sciences.
education: BS, Physics and Mathematics, University of Wisconsin-Oshkosh (1988)
early_career: Research scientist, NASA Ames Research Center (Intelligent Systems Division) - applied Bayesian analysis and machine learning to aerospace data
Developed information-theoretic methods for physical inference, published across physics and statistics journals

key_events.event: Completed PhD at University of Minnesota. Joined NASA Ames Research Center as research scientist in the Intelligent Systems Division, applying Bayesian methods to aerospace data analysis.
Appointed Editor-in-Chief of MDPI's Entropy journal. Joined faculty at University at Albany (SUNY) as physics professor.

Published 'Estimating Flight Characteristics of Anomalous Unidentified Aerial Vehicles' in Entropy (21:10, 939). The paper analyzed the Nimitz and other documented UAP encounters using physics, calculating accelerations of 100 to 1,000+ g with no sonic booms, thermal signatures, or air disturbance - concluding the observations represent technology far beyond known human capabilities.

Led the first academic instrumented field expedition for UAP detection through UAPx at Laguna Beach, California. The multi-sensor campaign collected environmental and electromagnetic data during the observation period.
Presented UAP physics research at the inaugural Sol Foundation Symposium at Stanford University, alongside Garry Nolan, Avi Loeb, Jacques Vallee, and other researchers.
Named as contributing affiliate to Avi Loeb's Galileo Project, co-authoring the scientific methodology paper for the project's multimodal ground-based observatory network.

Presented at the Sol Foundation 2024 Symposium in San Francisco. Affiliated with IFEX (Interdisciplinary Research Centre for Extraterrestrial Studies) at University of Wurzburg, connecting with Hakan Kayal's satellite-based anomaly detection program.

Published as lead author on 'The New Science of Unidentified Aerospace-Undersea Phenomena (UAP)' in Progress in Aerospace Sciences (arXiv:2502.06794), a flagship collaborative paper with 30+ co-authors including Matt Szydagis, Hakan Kayal, and Beatriz Villarroel, establishing a scientific framework for empirical UAP investigation.

Promoted to full professor at University at Albany. UAPx field expedition paper published as first author in Progress in Aerospace Sciences, representing the first peer-reviewed report of an academic instrumented UAP field campaign.

associated_people.name: Dr. Beatriz Villarroel
associated_people.relationship: Co-authors on the 2025 'New Science of UAP' paper in Progress in Aerospace Sciences. Both affiliated with IFEX and Sol Foundation; Villarroel's archival plate research and Knuth's field instrumentation represent complementary empirical approaches.

associated_people.name: Prof. Avi Loeb
associated_people.relationship: Knuth is a Galileo Project affiliate and co-authored the project's multimodal observatory methodology paper. Both pursue rigorous instrumented empirical approaches to UAP detection.

associated_people.name: Dr. Garry Nolan
associated_people.relationship: Co-speakers at Sol Foundation inaugural symposium (November 2023) and 2024 symposium. Both are central to the Sol Foundation's scientific advisory network.

associated_people.name: Dr. Jacques Vallee
associated_people.relationship: Co-speakers at Sol Foundation events. Both co-appear on the 2025 'New Science of UAP' arXiv paper framework.

disclosures.title: Estimating Flight Characteristics of Anomalous Unidentified Aerial Vehicles
disclosures.notes: Peer-reviewed physics analysis of documented UAP cases including the Nimitz encounter. Calculated accelerations of 100 to 1,000+ g with no accompanying sonic booms, thermal signatures, or air disturbance. Co-authored with Robert M. Powell and Peter A. Reali (Scientific Coalition for UAP Studies). DOI: 10.3390/e21100939.

disclosures.title: Scientific Investigation of UAP Using Multimodal Ground-Based Observatories
disclosures.notes: Galileo Project methodology paper co-authored with Loeb's team, describing the instrumented observatory network design for systematic UAP data collection. DOI: 10.1142/S2251171723400068.

disclosures.title: Sol Foundation Inaugural Symposium - UAP Physics and Field Research
disclosures.notes: Presented UAP flight characteristic analysis and UAPx field methodology alongside Garry Nolan, Avi Loeb, Jacques Vallee, and Chris Mellon.

disclosures.title: Sol Foundation 2024 Symposium - Instrumented UAP Research
disclosures.notes: Presented updated UAPx findings and the scientific case for empirical field-based UAP investigation.

disclosures.title: The New Science of Unidentified Aerospace-Undersea Phenomena (UAP)
disclosures.notes: Lead author on the flagship collaborative paper with 30+ co-authors including Matt Szydagis, Hakan Kayal, Beatriz Villarroel, and Jacques Vallee. Establishes a scientific framework for empirical UAP investigation and consolidates peer-reviewed findings across multiple research programs.

disclosures.title: Initial Results from the First Field Expedition of UAPx
disclosures.notes: Co-authored with Matt Szydagis, Kugielsky, and Levy. First peer-reviewed report of an academic instrumented UAP field campaign, documenting methodology and sensor data from the 2021 Laguna Beach expedition.

claims.claim: Analysis of documented UAP cases including the Nimitz encounter shows accelerations of 100 to 1,000+ g with no sonic booms, thermal signatures, or air disturbance - consistent with technology far beyond known human capabilities or requiring fundamentally different physics.

The 2021 UAPx Laguna Beach field expedition collected multi-sensor environmental and electromagnetic data during UAP observation windows, establishing proof-of-concept for academic instrumented field UAP research.

A collaborative scientific framework spanning physics analysis, archival astronomy, field instrumentation, and satellite detection can constitute a rigorous empirical research program for UAP investigation.

career_connections.relationship: Co-authors on 2025 New Science of UAP flagship paper; both affiliated with IFEX.
Galileo Project affiliate; co-authored observatory methodology paper.
Co-speakers at Sol Foundation inaugural and 2024 symposia.
Lead scientist of UAPx, conducting the first academic instrumented UAP field expeditions.
